name: Vickswood
description_short: Vickswood Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving employees of International Paper, Warren County and Yazoo County residents, and school system workers in Mississippi.
description_long:
Vickswood Federal Credit Union was established in October 1967 by eight employees of the International Paper Company in Redwood, Mississippi, with the goal of providing financial services to company employees and their families. Over decades, the credit union expanded its membership eligibility to include school system employees in Warren County, company employees, and eventually any resident, worker, student, or worshipper in Warren County and Yazoo County, Mississippi, as well as businesses and legal entities in those areas. Like all credit unions, Vickswood is a member-owned cooperative governed by a volunteer board of directors comprised entirely of member-owners who serve without compensation.

Vickswood offers a full range of consumer financial products including savings accounts, auto loans (as low as 4.99% APR), signature loans (7.00% APR), shared secured loans (2.99% APR), and recreational vehicle loans (5.99% APR). The credit union also runs seasonal promotions, such as a spring loan special offering $300, and youth-focused initiatives like Youth Month celebrations with in-lobby contests. Members can access online banking and enroll in accounts through their website.

What distinguishes Vickswood is its deep roots in the local Mississippi community, with membership eligibility explicitly tied to geographic regions and specific employers rather than serving a purely national market. The credit union emphasizes member financial education through its mission statement focused on "achieving personal financial success" and maintains strong communication about fraud prevention, explicitly warning members that the institution will never request online banking credentials or sensitive card information unsolicited. As a federally chartered credit union, Vickswood member deposits are insured up to $250,000 by the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUA).

Vickswood represents a traditional community-focused credit union model with competitive loan rates and member-centric governance. However, the website provides limited detail on fees, minimum deposit requirements, savings account interest rates, or specific terms for its loan products. The geographic limitation of membership to Mississippi counties may restrict access for those outside the service area, and the credit union's small size compared to larger financial institutions may result in fewer branch locations and less extensive digital banking features.
services: Shared secured loans (credit builder loans backed by savings deposits)
Youth Month programs and in-lobby promotions
NCUA deposit insurance (up to $250,000 per member)
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.99% APR
Shared secured loans available at 2.99% APR for credit-building members
Member deposits insured up to $250,000 by NCUA (U.S. government backing)
Member-owned cooperative structure with volunteer board that serves member interests, not stockholders
Democratic governance: each member gets one vote at annual meetings regardless of account balance
Strong fraud prevention messaging and transparent communication about phishing/social engineering threats
No stockholder profit motive; earnings returned to members through better rates and lower fees
Seasonal promotions like $300 Spring Loan Special and Youth Month incentives
cons: Membership limited to specific geographic areas (Warren County and Yazoo County, Mississippi) and employers; not available nationally
Website does not disclose savings account interest rates, making rate comparison difficult
No information provided about account fees, minimum deposit requirements, or loan origination fees
Limited details on digital banking capabilities or mobile app features beyond mention of online banking enrollment
Small credit union size may mean fewer physical branch locations and less extensive service offerings compared to larger institutions
best_for: Warren County and Yazoo County, Mississippi residents seeking community-focused financial services with competitive loan rates
International Paper Company employees and their immediate family members
School system employees in Warren County looking for member-owned financial products
Credit union members who prioritize governance transparency and member-owned cooperative structure over corporate banking
diagnosis: Vickswood is best for Mississippi residents and employees in its service territory who value community-focused, member-owned banking with competitive loan rates and transparent governance. The primary caveat is geographic restriction—membership is limited to Warren County and Yazoo County, Mississippi, plus specific employer groups, making it inaccessible to those outside these boundaries.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Vickswood — Credit Union in Vicksburg, MS | CreditDoc
meta_description: Vickswood — NCUA-insured credit union in Vicksburg, MS. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
